architecture_summary: CSWin Transformer is a hierarchical vision Transformer backbone that replaces full self-attention with Cross-Shaped Window self-attention, computing attention in horizontal and vertical stripes in parallel, and adds Locally-enhanced Positional Encoding (LePE) as a parallel module to the attention branch.

novel_contributions: Cross-Shaped Window self-attention that computes self-attention in horizontal and vertical stripes in parallel
Locally-enhanced Positional Encoding (LePE) that operates on projected values as a parallel module
A hierarchical vision Transformer backbone named CSWin Transformer for general-purpose vision tasks
Dynamic stripe width across layers to balance modeling capability and computation cost

key_claims.claim: CSWin Transformer is an efficient and effective Transformer-based backbone for general-purpose vision tasks, using cross-shaped window self-attention and LePE to enlarge receptive field while limiting computation cost.

CSWin-B achieves 85.4% Top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K without any extra training data or label, 53.9 box AP and 46.4 mask AP on COCO, and 52.2 mIOU on ADE20K, surpassing previous state-of-the-art Swin Transformer backbone by +1.2, +2.0, +1.4, and +2.0 respectively under similar FLOPs.
By further pretraining on ImageNet-21K, CSWin achieves 87.5% Top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K and 55.7 mIoU on ADE20K.

CSWin self-attention splits multi-heads into parallel groups and applies horizontal and vertical stripe attention simultaneously, which enlarges the attention area of each token within one Transformer block efficiently.
LePE adds positional information directly onto the linearly projected values as a parallel module, and is designed to support varying input resolutions.
CSWin-T, CSWin-S, and CSWin-B outperform previous state-of-the-art vision Transformers on ImageNet-1K classification, COCO detection, and ADE20K segmentation under similar FLOPs and model size.
The paper reports that the parallel multi-head grouping strategy is more efficient and effective than sequential attention variants, especially on downstream tasks.
